revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hikone, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kōjinyama Kofun | native_name = 荒神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kojin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13,150 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]] [[Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hikone, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kōjinyama Kofun | native_name = 荒神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kojin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|14|6.91|N|136|11|52.35|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13,150 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.

The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Kojin-yama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hikone.lg.jp/material/files/group/4/21_kojinyama_kofun.pdf Hikone city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/2042819.pdf Shiga Prefecture official home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers

==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kojin-yama Kofun]][[en:Kōjinyama Kofun]][[ja:荒神山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28608900]] Wikidata name: Kōjin-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hikone, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kōjinyama Kofun | native_name = 荒神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kojin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.235253|N|136.197875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13,150 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Kojin-yama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hikone.lg.jp/material/files/group/4/21_kojinyama_kofun.pdf Hikone city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/2042819.pdf Shiga Prefecture official home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Hikone, Shiga]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]
[[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.

color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Hikone, Shiga|Hikone]] [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] and was also the location of the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from [[:en:Kawase Station|Kawase Station]] on the JR [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Kojin-yama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.hikone.lg.jp/material/files/group/4/21_kojinyama_kofun.pdf Hikone city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/2042819.pdf Shiga Prefecture official home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} and was also the location of the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|WD=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} and was also the location of the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|WD=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|WD=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station|1=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|WD=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station|1=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kojin-yama Kofun]][[en:Kōjinyama Kofun]][[ja:荒神山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kojin-yama Kofun]][[en:Kōjinyama Kofun]][[ja:荒神山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kōjinyama Kofun]][[ja:荒神山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hikone, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kōjinyama Kofun | native_name = 荒神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kojin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.235253|N|136.197875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hikone|WD=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13,150 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Hikone, Shiga|en|Hikone, Shiga|WD=Q386635|lt=Hikone|1=Hikone, Shiga}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} and was also the location of the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|WD=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station|1=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|qid=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station|1=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|qid=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
;Overall length: 124 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 80 meter diameter x 16 meter high x 3 tiers ;"Neck" portion: 52 meters wide x 9 meters high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 61 meters wide x 53 meters long x 10 meters high x 3 tiers
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Hikone, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name =Kōjinyama Kofun | native_name = 荒神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kojin-yama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kōjinyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.235253|N|136.197875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hikone|qid=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13,150 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kōjinyama Kofun'''|荒神山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Hinatsu and Kiyosaki neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Hikone, Shiga|en|Hikone, Shiga|qid=Q386635|lt=Hikone|1=Hikone, Shiga}} {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2011.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218990 |title=荒神山古墳|trans-title= Kōjinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kōjinyama Kofun is located on the top of a ridge that descends from the summit of 284-meter Mount Kōjin, on the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|qid=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The surrounding area was once part of ''[[shōen]]'' controlled by [[Tōdai-ji]] in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} and was also the location of the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}} Kōjinyama Castle. The mountain also contains more than ten other known ''kofun'' Kofuns, forming the "Kōjinyama Kofun cluster".
The tumulus has a total length of 124 meters and is thus the second largest in Shiga Prefecture. The exterior of the tumulus was surveyed from 2002 to 2007. It was discovered that the mound had been constructed in three tiers, and that it had originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Numerous shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were recovered from the vicinity. These include cylindrical, "morning glory-shaped", and figurative (shaped like houses, animals or people) ''haniwa''. The interior has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are unknown, but the tumulus is believed to date from the early Kofun period (end of the 4th century AD) from the shape of the mound and type of ''haniwa''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the posterior circular portion has an indentation and slabs of stone are scattered around the site, it is assumed that the tumulus had a pit-type burial chamber which was looted in antiquity. The excavated ''haniwa'' are collectively designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Hikone City.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/235581 |title=荒神山古墳出土埴輪 |trans-title= Haniwa excavated from Kojinyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> They are kept at the {{nihongo|Kaikoku Memorial Hall|開国記念館|Kaikoku Kinenkan}} in Hikone city.
The tumulus is located about ten minutes by car from {{ill|Kawase Station|en|Kawase Station|qid=Q614782|lt=Kawase Station|1=Kawase Station}} on the JR {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|qid=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The {{nihongo|Inabe site|稲部遺跡|Inabe iseki}}, which is about three kilometers south of the Kōjinyama Kofun, contains the ruins of a very large building with a floor area of 188 square meters, was contemporary to the tumulus, and it is speculated that there is some connection between these sites. Also, the temple of {{nihongo|Enjuzen-ji|延寿禅寺||}} at the foot of Mount Kōjin has a large stone wheel made of green {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}. While its provenance is uncertain, similar stone wheels are known to have been used to seal the burial chambers in ''kofun'' tumuli, and both the composition of the stone and its workmanship coincide with the stone fragments of the burial chamber found near the tumulus.
